name: Garlic Pancakes with Ham Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together 12 tablespoons (1-1/2 sticks) butter and sage; reserve.
2. Remove as much outer skin from garlic as possible without piercing the cloves' covering. Set garlic in saucepan, cover with water, and boil gently for about 45 minutes or until cloves are quite soft. Remove from heat and let cool enough to handle.
3. Squeeze each clove of cooked garlic by grasping at the tip and pulling down toward the base. Beat the garlic with a fork until smooth.
4. Add the garlic and the same amount (at least one cup) flour, eggs, oil, and 1-1/2 cups milk to make pancake batter. Mix minced garlic into the batter and set aside.
5. Melt remaining 4 tablespoons butter and keep warm.
6. In a saucepan, add 6 tablespoons of sage butter. Add 6 tablespoons of flour to make a roux and cook at medium temperature, stirring frequently until browned.
7. Meanwhile, in a skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of sage butter and heat through without burning. Add ham and let it warm up.
8. When the roux is nicely browned, add the remaining 2 cups milk. Allow to thicken by stirring frequently.
9. Add ham and skillet drippings; mix to make ham sauce. Keep warm.
10. Heat a griddle or clean skillet and grease lightly with a bit of remaining sage butter.
11. Drop batter by spoonfuls onto the griddle to make silver dollar-sized pancakes.
12. Serve with melted butter and ham sauce. Makes 2 dozen pancakes.
NOTE: Cooking the garlic until it is soft changes its flavor from pungent to sweet and nut-like, which adds a pleasant compliment to the smokiness of the ham sauce.
